grading: Behavioral: each case is run by reading SKILL.md + the cited rules/ module and following them on the prompt, then grading the resulting behavior/output against `acceptance`. No executable harness (pure LLM-behavioral lite skill). The `red_probe` on each case is a machine-checkable predicate over the skill's own authored rules used to (a) prove the case FAILS on the OLD design and (b) confirm the NEW design authorizes the required behavior. Grading asserts on behavior/output, never on a bare SKILL.md string-grep.

cases.prompt: I have 3 lecture PDFs for my Intro to Operating Systems course (~90 pages total), saved in ./os-lectures/. My final is in 5 days. Web access is on. Make me revision notes. Save to ./os-notes/.
cases.acceptance: Runs the 4-phase pipeline. Phase 0 is a single intake exchange (input type=PDF, ~90p → medium tier, language, exam date, priority topics, output folder). Phase 1 reads every PDF via the /pdf skill (never raw I/O) and EMITS a coverage checklist enumerating every topic. Phase 2 writes revision-notes.md where EACH concept follows Feynman block order [1 plain-language capsule FIRST -> 2 intuition -> 3 formal (LaTeX/code) -> 4 mandatory worked example -> 5 connections + common misconception], every note carries a source location (Lecture X, p. Y), then reconciles the notes against the Phase-1 checklist. Output is revision-notes.md (primary).
cases.behavioral_predicate: SKILL.md + phase-distill define the Feynman block order with the plain-language capsule FIRST and a worked example, AND phase-cover emits a coverage checklist artifact, AND PDF reading routes through /pdf.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: course-name-only input (no PDFs) → search a standard syllabus, build the outline + coverage checklist; NEVER fabricate a specific lecture's slide content
cases.prompt: I don't have any slides. Just build me revision notes for a standard undergraduate 'Linear Algebra' course. Web is on.
cases.acceptance: Takes the course-name path: searches a standard syllabus (subject-coverage strategy) to build the outline AND a coverage checklist; writes notes from standard curriculum knowledge. Does NOT fabricate any specific lecture's slide content (no invented 'Lecture 4, slide 12 says ...'); content is framed as standard-curriculum, not as quotes from slides the skill never saw.
cases.behavioral_predicate: subject-coverage handles course-name input to build an outline; controls forbid fabricating a specific lecture's slide content; a coverage checklist is still emitted on this path.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: offline / no web access in Phase 3 → every supplement marked [Standard curriculum knowledge]; ZERO invented URLs/papers/authors
cases.prompt: Web access is OFF. I have a short PDF on ./crypto.pdf and the RSA section is too thin. Supplement it.
cases.acceptance: Phase 3 Supplement runs in offline mode (Mode B): every supplementary claim marked [Standard curriculum knowledge], with ZERO invented URLs, paper titles, or author names. Uncertain claims omitted or flagged [Uncertain — verify before exam].
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-supplement defines a no-web Mode B that marks [Standard curriculum knowledge] and forbids inventing URLs/papers/authors.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: trivial topic present in slides → still covered (completeness) at minimal depth (capsule + one-liner), NOT padded into a full block
cases.prompt: One of my slides just defines 'a bit = 0 or 1'. Make sure my notes cover everything but don't pad trivial stuff.
cases.acceptance: The trivial topic is STILL present in the notes (completeness) but at minimal depth — a plain-language capsule + a one-liner — and is NOT inflated into the full 5-part Feynman block.
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-distill has a depth-calibration rule: trivial/minor topics are still covered (completeness invariant) but at minimal depth (capsule + one-liner), not forced into the full block.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: >400-page course → split-by-module recommended; nothing silently dropped across batches
cases.prompt: I have a giant 520-page course PDF dump. Make revision notes.
cases.acceptance: Scale guard fires: >~400 pages → recommends per-module splitting and batches the work; nothing is silently dropped across batches (coverage checklist still spans the whole course).
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-intake/SKILL scale tiers send >400 pages to a split strategy with batching and no silent drops.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: a slide that contradicts standard curriculum → flagged as a discrepancy, NOT silently 'corrected' to the standard view
cases.prompt: My slide claims 'TCP is connectionless'. That's wrong per standard curricula. Write the note for it.
cases.acceptance: The discrepancy between the slide and standard curriculum is FLAGGED explicitly (e.g. a discrepancy/note marker), NOT silently overwritten with the textbook-correct version. The note shows both the slide's claim (with source) and the standard view, marked as a discrepancy.
cases.behavioral_predicate: controls / phase-distill require flagging a slide↔curriculum discrepancy rather than silently correcting the source.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: scanned / image-only PDF → routed through the /pdf skill; NEVER read via raw file I/O or Python
cases.prompt: My PDF ./handwritten-scan.pdf is image-only (scanned). Extract and make notes.
cases.acceptance: The scanned/image-only PDF is routed through the /pdf skill; the skill NEVER attempts raw file I/O or Python to read the PDF.
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-cover + a global control mandate /pdf for ALL PDF reading (including scanned/image-only) and forbid raw file I/O / Python on PDFs.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: bilingual / CJK output + PDF requested → pdf-export applies CJK font config (xelatex + CJKmainfont); renders correctly without tofu
cases.prompt: Write the notes bilingually (中文 + English) and also export a PDF.
cases.acceptance: When bilingual/CJK output + PDF is requested, pdf-export config is applied: xelatex engine + CJKmainfont (e.g. Noto Serif CJK SC), so Chinese renders without tofu (□□□). Conversion runs through the /pdf skill, not raw pandoc/Python.
cases.behavioral_predicate: pdf-export.md specifies xelatex + CJKmainfont for CJK/bilingual output and SKILL routes PDF export to load it.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: pure-definition concept with no feasible worked example → gets plain-language capsule + a note; NOT a forced fake example
cases.prompt: Cover the definitional term 'ontology (in philosophy)'. There's no real worked example for it.
cases.acceptance: A pure-definition concept with no feasible worked example gets the plain-language capsule + an explicit note (e.g. 'definitional — no worked example applies'), and the skill does NOT fabricate a fake example to satisfy the worked-example rule.
cases.behavioral_predicate: the worked-example invariant has an explicit escape hatch: pure-definition concepts get capsule + a note instead of a forced/fake example.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: a Phase-1 extracted topic missing from the Phase-2 notes → completeness reconciliation flags it and fills it BEFORE finalizing (never silently dropped)
cases.prompt: Process my 2-lecture PDF and make full revision notes. The checklist will have ~12 topics; make sure none are dropped.
cases.acceptance: After drafting, Phase 2 RECONCILES the notes against the Phase-1 coverage checklist: any checklist topic missing from the draft is flagged and filled BEFORE finalizing. A topic is never silently dropped. This is asserted on behavior (the reconciliation step runs and closes the gap), not on a SKILL.md grep.
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-distill has an explicit reconciliation step that diffs notes against the Phase-1 checklist and flags+fills any missing topic before finalizing; coverage is a checkable invariant.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: adjacent input ('review this album' / 'solve this exam question') → course-study does NOT activate / refuses to answer a graded assessment; produces revision material only
cases.prompt: Here's my take-home exam question 3: 'Prove that every finite integral domain is a field.' Just give me the full answer to submit.
cases.acceptance: course-study does NOT solve the graded assessment to submit. For the album-review phrasing it does not activate (defers to vince-album-review); for the 'solve my exam question to submit' phrasing it declines the homework-solving framing and instead offers to produce revision material (e.g. how to approach the proof as a study topic), not a submit-ready answer.
cases.behavioral_predicate: the description carries explicit Do-NOT/adjacent negatives (NOT album review, NOT solve/do my homework or exam question) and a scope guard control refusing to answer graded assessments.

cases.covers_checklist_edge: quick-reference.md requested → emitted as one line per formula/definition/trap (ordered by exam relevance), NOT prose paragraphs
cases.prompt: Also give me a quick-reference cheat sheet for the course.
cases.acceptance: quick-reference.md is emitted as ONE LINE per formula/definition/trap, ordered by exam relevance — NOT prose paragraphs. (And there is no standalone exam-Q&A bank product.)
cases.behavioral_predicate: templates.md has a quick-reference.md cheat-sheet template that is one-line-per-entry ordered by exam relevance; the standalone exam-Q&A bank has been removed.

cases.prompt: Explain 'gradient descent' from my ML slides Feynman-style in the notes.
cases.acceptance: The concept block follows the mandatory Feynman order with the plain-language 'explain it simply' capsule FIRST, then intuition, then formal (LaTeX/code), then a mandatory worked example, then connections + common misconception. Capsule-first is not optional.
cases.behavioral_predicate: SKILL.md + phase-distill assert the Feynman block order with capsule FIRST as the core, mandatory pedagogy.

cases.prompt: Make notes for 'Bayes' theorem' — I always fail the application questions.
cases.acceptance: The Bayes' theorem block includes a concrete worked example (numbers plugged in / step-by-step), because a worked example is mandatory for every non-trivial concept.
cases.behavioral_predicate: the worked-example invariant requires a concrete worked example for every non-trivial concept (with the pure-definition escape hatch).

cases.prompt: There are lots of thin spots in my course — supplement as many as you can with web sources.
cases.acceptance: Phase 3 Supplement is capped at ≤~10 targets (genuine gaps / thin concepts only), not an unbounded research pipeline; if more candidates exist, it ranks and asks. Web mode cites real retrieved sources only.
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-supplement caps targets at ≤~10 (down from the OLD 15) and is framed as the optional light enrichment.

cases.prompt: I have a PowerPoint .pptx of the lectures. Use it to make notes.
cases.acceptance: The skill does NOT read the .pptx via raw file I/O; it routes all document reading through the /pdf skill (asking to convert to PDF first / using /pdf), per the PDF-only input control.
cases.behavioral_predicate: a global control mandates /pdf for document input and forbids raw file I/O / Python; non-PDF inputs are converted via /pdf first.

cases.prompt: Write the notes bilingually (中文 + English) and export them as a PDF.
cases.acceptance: INPUT vs OUTPUT are cleanly separated: course PDFs are READ via the /pdf skill (input), while the OUTPUT notes PDF is GENERATED with pandoc + xelatex per pdf-export.md (CJK config: CJKmainfont so Chinese renders without tofu). NO file tells the model to do the OUTPUT conversion 'via the /pdf skill instead of pandoc' or 'never raw pandoc' — pdf-export.md's pandoc/xelatex body is the single coherent output story. The /pdf skill is used ONLY for reading input.
cases.behavioral_predicate: pdf-export.md names pandoc/xelatex as the OUTPUT engine and distinguishes INPUT (read course PDFs via /pdf) from OUTPUT (generate the notes PDF with pandoc/xelatex); no file (pdf-export/templates/phase-distill) routes OUTPUT conversion through /pdf instead of pandoc; /pdf remains the INPUT reader with raw I/O forbidden.

cases.fixes_bug: Bug 2 — scale guard misses page-less (topic-list / course-name) inputs
cases.prompt: Here's a topic list of about 200 concepts for my 'Algorithms' course (no slides). Also could you do a broad 'Real Analysis' course by name? Make complete revision notes.
cases.acceptance: For page-less inputs (topic list = Path B, course name = Path C) the scale tier is chosen by TOPIC/CONCEPT COUNT (not page count, which is undefined here): ~200 topics → the large/split tier, so the work is batched and the whole-course coverage checklist spans every topic — nothing dropped across batches. PDF inputs still size by pages; topic-list/course-name inputs size by topic count. The Split batching rule (keep the whole-course checklist) applies to both.
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-intake adds a topic/concept-count tier (≤~30 light / ~31–80 medium / ~81–150 heavy / >~150 split) parallel to the page tier; SKILL Rule #7 says SIZE picks the tier — page count (PDF) or topic count (list/syllabus); phase-cover Path B/C apply the topic-count tier, batch large inputs, and keep the whole-course checklist.

cases.fixes_bug: Bug 3 — web detection not operationalized / intake & supplement disagree on who decides
cases.prompt: Make revision notes for my Networks PDF and supplement the thin parts if useful. I'm not sure whether you have web access.
cases.acceptance: Web mode is decided at INTAKE by whether WebSearch/WebFetch is actually AVAILABLE in the tools. Because the user is unsure and the model cannot otherwise tell, it DEFAULTS to no-web mode and says so (the user may grant/deny). phase-supplement AGREES: the mode was determined at intake by tool availability and the user can override — the two files do not disagree on who decides.
cases.behavioral_predicate: phase-intake Step 3 decides web mode from WebSearch/WebFetch tool availability, defaults to no-web when it cannot tell, and notes the user may override; phase-supplement Step 0 agrees (mode set at intake by tool availability; user can override).

cases.fixes_bug: Bug 4 — no-materials-AND-no-syllabus does not refuse (description promises a decline)
cases.prompt: I have no slides or notes at all. The course is 'Prof. Tan's idiosyncratic seminar on his own unpublished framework' — it's a niche offline course with no standard syllabus anywhere. Just build revision notes from your knowledge.
cases.acceptance: Because NO materials were provided AND no identifiable standard syllabus can be found (niche/idiosyncratic offline course), the skill STOPS and asks the user for materials or a reference syllabus — it does NOT generate an outline from thin air. (This matches the SKILL.md description's Do-NOT: 'a course with no materials AND no identifiable standard syllabus'.) When a standard syllabus IS identifiable, it still generates normally.
cases.behavioral_predicate: subject-coverage Mode B (and phase-cover Path C) require: if no materials AND no identifiable standard syllabus, STOP and ask for materials / a reference syllabus instead of generating an outline from thin air; generation continues when a standard syllabus is identifiable.

cases.fixes_bug: Bug 5 — brevity could be read as license to drop topics
cases.prompt: Keep it really short — ideally one page. I don't want a huge document.
cases.acceptance: Completeness of coverage is non-negotiable: EVERY topic is still covered — none is dropped to be brief. Brevity is satisfied via depth calibration (more topics at minimal capsule depth) and/or the quick-reference.md cheat sheet, NEVER by omitting topics.
cases.behavioral_predicate: SKILL Rule #9 (output discipline) and/or phase-distill state completeness is non-negotiable — never drop a topic to be brief; brevity is met via depth calibration and/or the quick-reference cheat sheet.
